name: claim->govern->grant->exec->sign
intent: The full governed syscall: authenticate a principal, govern a value-free claim into a hashed plan, then per-step gate (plan-hash+upstream+ACL), delegate confined execution to exod, verify its signed result, and record server-owned provenance.

name: cooperative step path
intent: Default mode: the agent runs each step client-side; govd grants on plan-hash+upstream+live-ACL, records the grant, then accepts a status-only step_result only if it follows that grant (unforgeable upstream ledger).

name: delegated step path
intent: Containment mode: govd atomically claims the step (at-most-once), materializes a confined workspace, mints a signed grant, and hands it to exod which runs confined and signs the authoritative result; agent self-reports are rejected.

name: ACL M1 attestation
intent: Operator signs an actor->ACL attestation off-body; govd recomputes acl_sha from live registry fields and binds it into the delegated grant so exod re-derives and joins it, enforcing the ceiling off-node against a compromised govd.

name: plan compose + deadlock check
intent: Before blessing: authenticity-verify the registry against its index, structurally and via TLA+/TLC check the blueprint for deadlock, then compile the value-free plan and pin its sha256 (the thing oversight monitors).

name: chip acquire + validate boot gate
intent: Before govd serves: acquire the skillChip (local baked or live cloud clone with leak-free token), validate every skill index + the chip manifest, and pass provenance to govd or exit non-zero (the engine never serves an unchecked cartridge).

name: HA active-passive failover
intent: Two govd over one shared store: exactly one holds a mutually-exclusive single-writer lease (mirror gated on held); on active death the lease expires and the standby acquires it and resumes, with nonce replay guards making re-sent steps idempotent.

name: monitor SSE feed
intent: Token-gated dashboard push: re-derive the value-free snapshot, push a data event only when content (sans the volatile now stamp) changes, paginated and bounded against resource accumulation.

name: plan-hash inconsistency investigation
intent: When a presented plan_sha mismatches the blessed one, investigate by PLAIN TEXT DIFF (never execute/source the submitted plan); return the diff to the caller and record only a line-count in the server audit.

name: claim -> govern -> grant -> exec -> sign
intent: govd mints a grant; exod (separate principal) verifies grant+ACL+closure off-node, resolves secrets step-side, runs the step in the kernel sandbox, signs the only step-result the ledger will trust, and the recorder writes it only if exod's signature and run-binding verify.

name: ACL M1 attestation (off-node ceiling)
intent: When a grant carries acl_sha and an operator acl-issuer is pinned, exod requires a valid operator attestation that joins the grant by acl_sha and independently re-runs acl_allows, so a compromised govd cannot widen a token's ceiling.

name: ACL M2 token-possession proof (run<->token binding)
intent: When the operator binds a client proof pubkey into the attestation, exod requires a matching per-step token proof, so a compromised govd relaying a more-privileged token's attestation cannot misattribute the run.

name: closure TOCTOU gate
intent: Before a run, exod re-derives the digest of every file in the materialized SNIP closure and requires it to match the grant's pins, refusing a post-grant porter/core swap or a smuggled sibling at time of use.

name: sandbox tier -> backend monotone floor
intent: The grant's sandbox tier selects the confinement backend as a monotone floor over the operator's --backend (community forces gVisor), and a non-enforceable backend fails closed rather than running unconfined.

name: backend seam-equivalence proof
intent: bwrap and runsc render the same value-free SandboxProfile; confinement() extracts the full boundary from each rendering so the community (gVisor) backend is proven to never weaken the bwrap boundary.

name: capability manifest exact-match
intent: A sandbox is run only if its tier permits the request and its materialized binds provably equal the declared manifest exactly, so a tampered profile can never widen reach.

name: double-blind secret resolution
intent: exod holds the vault and resolves grant-authorized credentials into the confined step's env after --clearenv, so the secret reaches the step but the agent's environ carries zero secret bytes.

name: forged-status refusal at the ledger
intent: The ledger accepts a status only when exod's signature verifies and it is bound to this run; a forged self-report is refused and recorded as forged_status_refused evidence.

name: SV-3 kernel red-team corpus
intent: Each behaviour mounts a real sandbox or channel attack through exod with no in-process scan and asserts the kernel/crypto boundary refuses it while a benign oracle is still accepted.

name: canonical-bytes path
intent: Serialize any JSON value to the one RFC 8785 JCS byte sequence and its sha256; every cyberware hash and signed payload is this and only this.

name: native DSSE sign/verify (Ed25519 pure)
intent: cwp-native signing: PAE over JCS canonical bytes, deterministic Ed25519, into a DSSE envelope tagged with the stable keyid; verify reverses it.

name: cosign Ed25519ph interop
intent: Bridge to sigstore/cosign: Ed25519ph over the identical PAE via the OpenSSL CLI, so cwp envelopes verify in cosign and vice versa — the base of all publisher signing.

name: in-toto blob attestation
intent: Bind a blob's sha256 subject to a predicate as an in-toto Statement and Ed25519ph-DSSE-sign its canonical bytes.

name: KeyStore custody seam
intent: One custody contract (generate/public_raw/sign/keyid/verify) across a file backend and a non-exportable HSM-shaped backend, validated by the shared contract suite.

name: publisher release signing (SV-4)
intent: Sign the chip+skill release manifest under the publisher key and refuse any unsigned/tampered/drifted release at all three entry points against the pinned TUF root.

name: signed revocation feed (fail-closed)
intent: A monotonic publisher-signed feed names what must stop running; a consumer refuses any listed artifact and fails closed when the feed is stale, rolled back, or forged.

name: key rotation drill (overlap + cross-sign + revoke)
intent: Rotate a signing key with a both-keys-verify overlap, a cross-signed rotation record proving continuous authority, then a clean revocation of the old key via the feed gate.

name: engine attestation + mutual handshake (SV-4)
intent: Publisher-sign the reproducible engine digest; both principals re-measure the live binary and verify under the pinned root, failing closed on any one-byte tamper and binding the running engine to the signed release.

name: Ledger-v2 chain verification
intent: Recompute a prev-hash provenance chain via JCS link digests and validate genesis, single-origin, seq contiguity, tamper, and transplant binding.

name: append->mirror->index->reconcile (provenance write path)
intent: Govd hands value-free snapshots to the mirror; one worker writes the chain (artifact of record) FIRST then the derived index, and the reconciler later re-derives the chain and proves index == chain.

name: decision mirror
intent: A verdict summary is enqueued, written to the decisions chain, then indexed into idx_decision.

name: value-free projection (secret exclusion, defense in depth)
intent: Allowlist projections strip everything but known-safe keys so the session token / wrapper / model-check log can never ride into the chain or index.

name: single-writer lease (P5-T04 active-passive, no split-brain)
intent: Atomic store-enforced lease elects one writer; the mirror's drain consults lease_holder via the guard and DROPS+records any shared-chain write attempted without the lease, so a partitioned ex-active can never append behind the new active.

name: reconcile divergence taxonomy + transplant guard
intent: Each cycle re-verifies the chain, re-asserts the out-of-band expected origin (set_origin/get_origin) to defeat forged genesis, recomputes digests, and set-diffs vs the index — alarming on gap_below_head/index_ahead/digest_mismatch/chain_broken/origin_missing while treating index_behind as benign lag (optionally repaired).

name: StoreBackend contract suite (both backends, one identical proof)
intent: Hermetic selftest builds a real prev-hash chain, mirrors it, and proves the six contract properties (interface conformance, round-trip, idempotent replay, reconcile-exact, torn-tail-safe, Postgres-inert-until-configured) that every adapter must pass.

name: backend selection (inert-until-keyed)
intent: Operator config picks Postgres iff a dsn_file/dsn is wired (else local sqlite-WAL); the Postgres tier stays INERT — every op returns 'unconfigured' — and the DSN is read lazily server-side and never echoed.

name: price->quote->fund->grant->settle (the priced-run settlement spine)
intent: Price a run from its value-free plan, sign+fund the quote in per-quote escrow, admit the grant only when funded, then pay out exactly once on a dual-signed validation:pass quote-bound receipt and release the dispute-window holdback.

name: price->charge->collect (the settle-time platform tax)
intent: Collect the transparent itemized tax (substrate/skill_author/marketplace) automatically at settle via a pluggable rail, refusing any split that does not re-sum to the total, idempotent on plan_sha.

name: credit-tier usage billing
intent: Operator tops up a prepaid balance once; each priced run debits its micro-tax internally as a zero-sum split with no per-call Stripe fee, refused as a structural gate if credits cannot cover it.

name: metered llm settlement
intent: Reconcile exod-attested token meters against the provider receipt, decide the settleable pass-through amount, then settle the llm step by its output-contract verdict - work paid on pass, initiator refunded on fail while provider+govd are still paid.

name: reward-ledger conservation + money<->work audit
intent: Every movement is a balanced zero-sum posting set; reward_verify proves the money trail and the dual-signed work trail are a clean bijection with no orphan settlement or unpaid authorized receipt.

name: adapter-boundary dust + escrow liveness
intent: At a cent-billing PSP boundary the sub-cent residue posts to a dust account and is swept (signed) to treasury; expired unsettled escrow auto-refunds to its funder so no escrow strands value past its bound.

name: throughput group-commit + resume
intent: Single-writer-per-currency batches commit all-or-nothing while maintaining running balances, and an O(accounts) checkpoint lets a restarted writer verify its resume point cheaply.

name: dispute lifecycle
intent: After settlement a run is disputed by posting a bond; an m-of-n WebAuthn-approved resolution either claws back the holdback to the disputer or forfeits the bond to the payee, recording reputation deltas.

name: FMV index + competitive award + royalty/self-bounty
intent: A manipulation-resistant FMV index prices work; bounties/auctions award one validated winner; verified-tier publishes pay an alchemy-lineage royalty; the security program pays external researchers through the same ledger.

name: SV-6 capstone close
intent: Settle cyberware's own redeemed milestones as internal-credit bounties through the full pipeline, seed the first FMV index from them, and emit a dual-signed TSA-anchored completion receipt that verifies offline - closing the ladder.

name: chip authenticity gate (per-skill -> chip -> verify)
intent: Pin every skill's files to a skill_sha and roll them into a chip_sha, then re-verify on-disk files match the committed indexes (CI drift gate).

name: manifest-authoritative load set (permit AND present)
intent: Resolve the runtime load set from the root manifest's permitted roster intersected with on-disk presence; a directory scan only bootstraps a fresh chip, never routes the load set.

name: value-free discovery catalog (compare by sha)
intent: Build a names+hashes-only catalog (skills, authenticity status, perk var-KEYS) so govd and agent registries compare by skill_sha without bodies crossing the wire.

name: cartridge compile -> seal -> load-verify
intent: Cut a standalone cartridge of exactly the declared skills with a fresh root manifest, then load-verify that each declared skill is present, authentic, and binds to the root chip_sha (undeclared dirs cannot ride along).

name: in-skill governed test (case.json proof)
intent: Run each perk's pinned case.json through the SAME governed compiler+executor pipeline in a hermetic dir, then check declared expectations (exit/outputs/contains/json).

name: skill scaffold (skeleton that already composes)
intent: Generate a new skill's L++ lifecycle blueprint, perks.json, contracts, and per-perk snippet stubs so the skeleton validates and composes out of the box.

name: self-monitor gate (building-is-running, M5)
intent: Grade the engine against redeemable evidence: deadlock-free blueprints, hash authenticity, no scaffold stubs, relocatable porters, and a mutation-coverage ratchet that may only rise.

name: compose->reconcile->verify->serve
intent: Multi-source compose: place each source's skills by namespace, HARD-ERROR on an exact ns:name dup (manual reconciliation), re-pin + full-verify the composed v2 chip, atomic-swap into place.

name: three-gate govern
intent: The claim is gated by THREE independent fail-closed ANDs: VALIDATE (the skill's files match its committed index), ACCESS-1 (the skill's own access.json policy), ACCESS-2 (the per-actor token ACL). Re-bound at step time by step_reauthorize.

name: fleet_tier hierarchy: declare->rank->discover->group
intent: The fleet_tier topology hierarchy (orthogonal to trust): nodes self-declare fleet_tier in the roster; fleetd carries it through _self_descriptor/_probe and rank-filters /fleet/find; fleetdash groups the side-nav by it. It is the axis cws-fleet:deploy contains against — a node may spawn only a STRICTLY-lower fleet_tier subagent with a content-identity (skill_sha) chip + ACL subset of its own.
